zh.Louth Park, Lincolnshire, England: 英國林可爾史爾盧斯 帕克
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Henry Vavasour II於1292年出生在英國越可爾哈茲列武德 白 塔德卡斯特時，他的父親Sir Henry Vavasour I26歲，母親Constance Mowbray24歲。他於1327年與Anabella FitzHugh結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1349年11月27日在英國英格蘭越可爾享年57歲，埋葬在英國林可爾史爾盧斯。

zh-hans.Hazlewood by Tadcaster, Yorkshire, Engla: 英国越可爾哈茲列武德 白 塔德卡斯特

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Henry Vavasour II于1292年出生在英国越可爾哈茲列武德 白 塔德卡斯特时，他的父亲Sir Henry Vavasour I26岁，母亲Constance Mowbray24岁。他于1327年与Anabella FitzHugh结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1349年11月27日在英国英格兰越可爾享年57岁，埋葬在英国林可爾史爾盧斯。

nameMeanings.name: Vavasour
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: status name for a feudal tenant ranking immediately below a baron, Old French vavas(s)our, vavasseur, Latin vassus vassorum ‘vassal of vassals’.

variants.name: Bavister
meanings: English: Titford suggests that this is a late variant of Vavasour . It would be a dissimilated form of Vavister and Vavester, variants of Vavasour that are evidenced in Lincs, also in Cambs, whence the name spread into Beds. Compare Willa Vavister, 1579 in IGI (Scopwick, Lincs); Thomas Vavister, 1615 in IGI (Cranwell, Lincs); Mary Vavester, 1708, Joseph Vavisor, 1738 in IGI (New Sleaford, Lincs).

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Japanese: written 井伊 ‘well’ and ‘that’, the name one of Japan's great daimyō families, originally based in Tōtōmi (now part of Shizuoka prefecture), later in Kōzuke (now Gunma prefecture) and Ōmi (now Shiga prefecture). Other variants of the name substitute either character with similar sounding characters such as 飯 ‘cooked rice’, 居 ‘residence’, and so forth. It was formerly Romanized as Iyi. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Roman number II (meaning ‘the second’, i.e. ‘the younger’ of the two bearers of the name) is apparently also counted as a surname Ii.

History: Ii Naomasa (1561–1602) was awarded lands in Kōzuke and Ōmi for his service in the civil wars that won the shōgunate (military dictatorship) for Tokugawa Ieyasu. His family built the beautiful castle of Hikone, which still stands. His descendant Ii Naosuke (1815–1860) ruled Japan for two years as Tairō (Great Elder) and played a major part in signing treaties that opened Japan to foreign trade at the end of the Tokugawa Shōgunate. He was assassinated by adherents of the anti-foreign party.

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Henry Vavasour II was born in 1292, in Hazlewood by Tadcaster, Yorkshire, England, his father, Sir Henry Vavasour I, was 26 and his mother, Constance Mowbray, was 24. He married Anabella FitzHugh in 1327. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 27 November 1349, in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 57, and was buried in Louth, Lincolnshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Henry Vavasour II est né en 1292, à Hazlewood by Tadcaster, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Sir Henry Vavasour I, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Constance Mowbray, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Anabella FitzHugh en 1327. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 27 novembre 1349, à Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 57 ans, et a été enterré à Louth, Lincolnshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Henry Vavasour II 1292 in Hazlewood by Tadcaster, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Henry Vavasour I 26 und seine Mutter Constance Mowbray 24. Er hat 1327 Anabella FitzHugh geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 27. November 1349 im Alter von 57 Jahren in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Louth, Lincolnshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Henry Vavasour II è nato il nel 1292 nel Hazlewood by Tadcaster, Yorkshire, England, il suo padre, Sir Henry Vavasour I, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Constance Mowbray, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Anabella FitzHugh nel 1327. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 27 novembre 1349, nel Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 57 anni, e fu sepolto a Louth, Lincolnshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Henry Vavasour IIが1292年にイギリスヨークシュアイアハズルオオッド ビー タドカスターで生まれた時，父Sir Henry Vavasour Iは26歳，母Constance Mowbrayは24歳であった。1327年にAnabella FitzHughと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1349年11月27日にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアで57歳で死去し，イギリスリンコルンシュアイアロウスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Henry Vavasour II(이)가 1292년 영국 요르크실르 하즐루드 비 타드카스터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Henry Vavasour I(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Constance Mowbray(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1327년 Anabella FitzHugh(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1349년 11월 27일 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르에서 57세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 링코실르 루스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Henry Vavasour II nasceu em 1292, em Hazlewood by Tadcaster, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Henry Vavasour I, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Constance Mowbray, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Anabella FitzHugh em 1327. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 27 de novembro de 1349, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 57 anos, e foi sepultado em Louth, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Henry Vavasour II родился в 1292 г. в местности хазльвуд би тадкастир, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Henry Vavasour I было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Constance Mowbray, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Anabella FitzHugh в 1327 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 27 ноября 1349 г. в местности еркшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 57 лет и был похоронен в местности лоут, линколншир, Англия.
